one_time_description: TCDC transferred assets and operations to Tse'khene Development Limited Partnership on April 1, 2022, with note receivable of $6,558,812 recognized; also TCDC forest license transferred to SFP LP at net book value ($747,126)
economic_notes: Band operates multiple forestry, construction, logging and development entities under the Duz Cho and Tse'khene brands. Acquired 99.99% of Sekani Forest Products LP during the year. TCDC became inactive and transferred operations to TD LP.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 — note this is fiscal year 2023, not 2024. Auditor office is Prince George BC but firm name not explicitly stated in provided text; BDO Canada LLP inferred from address and context. Large and complex consolidation with multiple forestry/construction LPs accounted for under modified equity method. TCDC became inactive April 1, 2022 and transferred assets to TD LP creating large note receivable. Transfer to trust of $6,000,000 reduces annual surplus from $40,402,862 to $34,402,862. Net financial assets of $114,959,064 from statement of changes. Restricted cash includes $668,245 gaming funds plus $218,134 CMHC reserves. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $4,077,711 + Province of BC $9,781,106 + FNHA $4,394,605 + Government of Canada $1,302,145 + PGNAETA $266,878 + CMHC $235,912 + FNESC $69,484 + BC Hydro $483,325 = $20,611,166; OSR = $65,380,212 - $20,611,166 = $44,769,046. Expenses_enterprises mapped to Own Source schedule expenses. Balance sheet details not fully extracted from provided text.
